era_context: Late Predynastic Egypt (Naqada III period). Upper and Lower Egypt were distinct political and cultural entities. In Mesopotamia, the Early Dynastic I period was underway, with city-states competing for regional dominance. The Indus Valley Civilization was in its early urban phase.
geographic_coverage_gaps: All regions outside Egypt and Mesopotamia
disconfirming_evidence: No specific events are documented for this year. The period is entirely Predynastic, predating written Egyptian records.
historiographic_note: 3152 BCE is in the Predynastic period, for which no written records exist. Archaeological evidence from the Naqada III period (c. 3200–3000 BCE) shows increasing social complexity, craft specialization, and long-distance trade, but these developments cannot be assigned to specific years. The archaeological record is biased toward elite burials and monumental sites; the majority of the population leaves minimal trace.
